lines: Education is the passport to the future, for tomorrow belongs to those who prepare for it today.
The power of education extends beyond the development of skills we need for economic success. It can contribute to nation-building and reconciliation.
OTR Registration in NSP for ST Students
All Scheduled Tribe (ST) students seeking scholarships are hereby informed to complete One Time Registration (OTR) prior to scholarship application submission. Proceed to OTR registration portal.

The Government of Andhra Pradesh have decided to implementing the Post Metric Scholarships scheme, to all eligible students belonging to the SC, ST, BC, EBC (other than Kapu), Kapu, Minority, Differently Abled categories, on a saturation basis.
The disbursal of scholarship is categorised into two schemes:

To provide full fee reimbursement to every eligible student, i.e.Full Fee (Tuition Fee, Special Fees, Other Fees & Exam Fees) as defined in the G.O.Ms.No.66 SW(Edn) Dept., dated 8-9-2010 and as fixed by the competent authorities is reimbursed to all eligible students. ii.Full fee shall be credited to the respective College accounts on behalf of students.

To provide Rs.10,000/- per person to ITI students, Rs.15,000/- per person to Polytechnic students, Rs.20,000/- per person for other Degree and above courses per year to every eligible student for food and hostel expenses. MTF shall be credited into the respective account of the mother of the eligible student. In case of the demise or absence of the mother, the amount shall be credited to the account of the natural guardian of the student.
The students under the following categories are eligible for availing the Schemes.
All the students, pursuing Polytechnic, ITI and Degree & above level courses in Government/Aided/Private colleges, affiliated to State Universities / Boards.
Day scholar students, students in College Attached Hostels (CAH) and Department Attached Hostels (DAH).
of the aggregate attendance is mandatory for release of scholarships.
The students under the following categories are not eligible for availing the Schemes.
Studying in Private Universities / Deemed Universities.
The total family annual income to be less than or equal to
The total land holding of the family to be less than
acres both wet and dry land together.

No member of the family should be a government employee/ pensioner (all sanitary workers irrespective of their salary/ recruitment, are eligible. The Social Welfare Department shall develop a robust & fool-proof system of certification of parents of the beneficiaries under category of "Sanitary workers").
No member of the family should own a four-wheeler (Taxies/Tractors/Autos are exempted).
A family who owns no property or less than
Sft of built up area (Residential or Commercial) in urban areas is eligible.
No member of the family should be an income tax payee.
The following steps may be followed while applying for the PMS Scheme:
Collect Jnanabhumi Scholarship Application Form from the college.
Fill-in the JSAF form and submit the signed copy of the same to the College Principal

College Principal verifies the details filled in the JSAF and submits the same online through Jnanabhumi, at the time of admission.Upon successful completion of the same, the student receives an SMS confirming the successful submission of his application.

Student visits a Mee Seva Centre,provides his Jnanabhumi application id and Aadhaar number to gain access to his application form online.Student verifies the details, makes modifications if necessary and submits the application by giving his/her biometric authentication.

By way of registering a renewal application, the College Principal keys-in the student's admission date of the current year and Hall Ticket/Roll Number of the preceding year's examination.Upon successful completion of the same,the student receives an SMS confirming the successful submission of his/her renewal application.
Student views his/her application details and gives his/her biometric authentication at the college

G.O.Ms.No.115-SOCIAL WELFARE DEPARTMENT - Scholarships to every eligible student belonging to SC,ST,BC, EBC (other than Kapu), Kapu, Minority and Differently Abled categories for the Post Metric Courses ITI, Polytechnic and Degree & above level courses - Orders – Issued.

G.O.Ms.No.77 - Confinement of the scheme in case of PG courses to the students admitted under Convener quota offered by the State funded Universities and Government Degree/PG Colleges - Orders – Issued.

G.O.Ms.No.64 - Institutions not to insist on payment of fee from eligible students at the time of admissions and to conduct counselling to mothers of the Students to empower them to monitor the functioning of the colleges as well as the academic performance of their children - Orders – Issued.

G.O.MS.No. 81-Social Welfare Department - JnanaBhumi - Smart Portal for Integrated Delivery of Services relating to Education & Scholarships- Modifications in procedure & softwareImplementation of JnanaBhumi 2.0 during academic year 2019-20 - Orders - Issued.

G.O.Ms.No.18-Social Welfare Dept – Various stakeholders in implementation of the scheme Duties and timelines prescribed for each stakeholder- For effective implementation of the scheme - Orders - Issued
G.O.Ms.No.87-Social Welfare Department - Reimbursement of tuition fee – Operational Guidelines to change the mode of disbursement of RTF from 2024-2025 Academic year–Modification Orders - Issued.

Government of Andhra Pradesh is implementing Ambedkar Overseas Vidya Nidhi Scheme for providing financial assistance to eligible students belonging to SC/ST/BC/Minority/ EBC including Kapu students to pursue PG/Ph.D/MBBS courses in the academic year 2023-24, in any of the 21 broad category of subjects in top 50 Institutions/ Universities as per latest QS and Time Higher Education world rankings 2023.
Candidates should be native of A.P.
Maximum age should not exceed 35 years as on date of publication.
Family income shall not exceed Rs.8.00 lakhs per annum.
Only one candidate per family eligible.
Post Graduate: 60% marks in Engineering/Management/Sciences etc.
Candidate should file online application at:
The following documents are required for filing of applications:
Caste Certificate from concerned Village/Ward Secretariat
Income certificate from concerned Village/Ward Secretariat (physically certified by the District Collector)
Ration card of the family, if available
SSC certificate / Date of Birth certificate
TOEFL / IELTS / GRE / GMAT score sheet
Unconditional offer letter from foreign university
Latest Tax Returns of family members, if applicable
Nationalised Bank Account details of student
Self-certification regarding no prior financial assistance from State/Central Govt

The admissions to the Social Welfare hostels and the diet administration now happens through Jnanabhumi. The automation of these functions through Jnanabhumi, impacting 3.00 Lakh children spread across around 300 hostels, was preceded by a comprehensive revamping of the policy framework and the associated process re-engineering. While automation of the admission process has simplified the application process, the automation of diet administration helps monitor the compliance with policy stipulations, of the hostels in delivering the food items to the hostel boarders.

A similar exercise was undertaken to bring about reforms in the Social Welfare and other Welfare Residential Schools. The automation of the screening test, allotment of schools, admission and diet administration processes through Jnanabhumi, aligned with the renewed policy framework, has positively benefitted
residential institutions of all welfare departments.
Every year colleges need to get affiliated to the university mandatory.
Student Admissions / Sanctions starts only after the college gets affiliated to a University.
The release of MTF and RTF gets delayed if the affiliation delayed.
